competitive_position.summary: Competing primarily against GEP Worldwide (incumbent, P2P coverage, mid-2027 renewal) and Coupa (listed but not actively mentioned in buyer conversations). DFI is dissatisfied with GEP and sees Zycus as the AI-forward single-system alternative. Wedge is Agentic AI + S2C coverage + unified platform; activation is proof-of-value showcasing Merlin Intake and autonomous sourcing capacity recovery.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: DFI not happy with GEP and may look for replacement. GEP remains to cover P2P (does not cover S2C + Agentic AI).

champion_strength.source: Next Step 26 Mar, 2 Apr, 27 Apr (consistent engagement and internal advocacy); Contact Role: Evaluator
champion_strength.summary: Jacqueline Tuason is a strong, active champion with direct access to Jay Taylor (Decision Maker) and internal advocacy (AI-Roadmap alignment). However, champion power is limited to evaluation-phase influence and does not extend to Economic Buyer or budget authority. Champion strength will need to escalate to EB sponsorship to carry the deal through commercials.

sources: Contact Roles: Jay Taylor = Decision Maker, but no Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor role assigned
Next Step series (Jan-Jun 2026): no reference to CFO, CEO, or C-level budget authority
Inference: $360K multi-year SaaS + Agentic AI + services investment in enterprise retail context requires C-level or CFO approval
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. Jay Taylor (Group Non-Trade Procurement Director) holds the Decision Maker contact role and is driving the evaluation, but for a $360K multi-year platform investment replacing GEP across Source-to-Contract plus Agentic AI, final budget authority almost certainly rests with a CFO or C-level executive. The Next Step history references procurement-level stakeholders (Jay, Jacqueline, Antoine, Kevinson in Finance) but no mention of CFO, CEO, or C-suite budget sponsor. Without EB mapping, the deal cannot progress to commercials or proposal with confidence. The recommended move is to ask Jay directly who controls the budget approval for a $360K procurement platform investment and secure an introduction to that executive within the next two weeks, ideally before the proof-of-value exercise is scheduled.

recommended_moves: Secure concrete dates and agenda for the August-September proof-of-value workshop with Jay Taylor and Jacqueline Tuason, locking test-case scenarios, evaluation criteria weighting, and decision timeline in writing.

Ask Jay Taylor directly who controls budget approval for the $360K procurement platform investment and secure an introduction to that Economic Buyer (CFO or C-suite sponsor) within the next two weeks.

Re-engage the relevant stakeholder. (Head of IT Procurement, last contact 30 Jan, 5 months ago) to confirm his role in the evaluation, validate InfoSec approval process and timeline, and multi-thread the IT stakeholder layer.
